label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out which statement is NOT an advantage of the algorithm described in the text. First, I need to understand what the algorithm does. The article says the monitor watches for deceleration and distance from a light to guess which cars might break the Highway Code. It provides two seconds' warning if someone is about to cross a crossroads, which is enough time for other drivers to hit the brakes. Professor Jonathan How mentions that future "smart" cars might use this system to help drivers foresee and avoid accidents. The algorithm was tested in Virginia and accurately identified potential violators a couple of seconds before they reached a red light, allowing other drivers to react if alerted. It also mentions that compared to other models, this algorithm has fewer false alarms, which is important for systems guiding human drivers. Now, looking at the options: A) It will raise the alarm when the driver breaks the traffic rules. B) It usually produces fewer false alarms. C) It helps drivers avoid potential accidents by judging the threats. D) The monitor of this algorithm can judge the traffic condition from the lights of other vehicles. The text says the algorithm accurately identifies potential violators (i.e., those who might break the rules) and gives a warning. So A is an advantage. B is directly stated: "brought fewer false alarms" compared to others. C is also mentioned as the purpose: helps avoid accidents by foreseeing threats. D says the monitor can judge traffic conditions from the lights of other vehicles. The text says the monitor "watches deceleration and distance from a light," not the lights themselves.
